cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this problem step by step. The question is asking between what times Andrew could have gone to the clothing store, given the various activities he was doing at different times. Let me start by listing out all the information provided. First, Andrew woke up at 5 am. Then we have several witnesses who saw him doing different things during specific time intervals: - William saw Andrew playing tennis at the tennis court from 5am to 6am. - Jessica saw Andrew buying lunch at the deli from 6am to 9am. - Tiffany saw Andrew taking photos near the Leaning Tower of Pisa from 9am to 11am. - John saw Andrew waiting at the train station from 11am to 5pm. - Sarah saw Andrew reading at the library from 6pm to 9pm. Also, we know that the clothing store was closed after 9pm. So Andrew must have gone to the clothing store before 9pm. Now, the options given are: A) 9am to 11am B) 6pm to 9pm C) 11am to 5pm D) 5pm to 6pm The goal is to figure out which of these time intervals is a possible time when Andrew could have been at the clothing store, meaning he wasn't doing any of the other activities mentioned during that time. Now, let's see if there are any gaps in this timeline where Andrew wasn't doing any of these activities and could have gone to the clothing store. First, Andrew woke up at 5am, then immediately went to play tennis from 5am to 6am. Then from 6am to 9am, he was at the deli. So from 5am to 9am, he was doing something else. Then, from 9am to 11am, he was taking photos near the Leaning Tower of Pisa. So from 9am to 11am, he was occupied. From 11am to 5pm, he was at the train station.
